messages.content:
Goal: Publish /robots.txt with clear crawl rules

Issue: robots.txt not found

Fix: Create /robots.txt at the site root with explicit User-agent directives and allow/disallow rules for key paths. Ensure it is plain text and returns 200.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/robots-txt/SKILL.md

Docs: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9309

---

Goal: Publish a sitemap and reference it from robots.txt

Issue: sitemap.xml not found

Fix: Generate /sitemap.xml listing canonical URLs, keep it updated on publish, and reference it from /robots.txt.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/sitemap/SKILL.md

Docs: https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html

---

Goal: Include Link response headers for agent discovery (RFC 8288)

Issue: No Link headers found on homepage

Fix: Add Link response headers to your homepage that point agents to useful resources. For example: Link: </.well-known/api-catalog>; rel="api-catalog" to advertise your API catalog, or Link: </docs/api>; rel="service-doc" for API documentation. See RFC 8288 for the Link header format and IANA Link Relations for registered relation types.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/link-headers/SKILL.md

Docs: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8288, https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9727#section-3

---

Goal: Return HTML responses as markdown when agents request it

Issue: Site does not support Markdown for Agents

Fix: Enable Markdown for Agents so requests with Accept: text/markdown return a markdown version of your HTML response while HTML stays the default for browsers. Confirm the response uses Content-Type: text/markdown (and x-markdown-tokens if available).

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/markdown-negotiation/SKILL.md

Docs: https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/markdown-for-agents/

---

Goal: Add User-agent rules for AI crawlers like GPTBot, Claude-Web, and others

Issue: Cannot check AI rules without robots.txt

Fix: Add explicit User-agent entries for AI crawlers (GPTBot, OAI-SearchBot, Claude-Web, Google-Extended) with allow/disallow rules that match your policy.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/ai-rules/SKILL.md

Docs: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9309, https://developers.cloudflare.com/ai-crawl-control/

---

Goal: Declare AI content usage preferences with Content Signals in robots.txt

Issue: Cannot check Content Signals without robots.txt

Fix: Add Content-Signal directives to your robots.txt declaring preferences for ai-train, search, and ai-input. For example:
Content-Signal: ai-train=no, search=yes, ai-input=no

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/content-signals/SKILL.md

Docs: https://contentsignals.org/, https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-romm-aipref-contentsignals/

---

Goal: Publish an API catalog for automated API discovery (RFC 9727)

Issue: API Catalog returned HTML instead of JSON

Fix: Create /.well-known/api-catalog returning application/linkset+json with a "linkset" array. Each entry should include an "anchor" URL for the API and link relations for service-desc (OpenAPI spec), service-doc (documentation), and status (health endpoint). See RFC 9727 Appendix A for examples.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/api-catalog/SKILL.md

Docs: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9727, https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9264

---

Goal: Publish OAuth/OIDC discovery metadata so agents can authenticate with your APIs

Issue: No OAuth/OIDC discovery metadata found

Fix: If your site has protected APIs, publish /.well-known/openid-configuration (for OpenID Connect) or /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server (for pure OAuth 2.0) with your issuer, authorization_endpoint, token_endpoint, jwks_uri, and grant_types_supported. This allows AI agents to programmatically discover how to authenticate.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/oauth-discovery/SKILL.md

Docs: http://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html, https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8414

---

Goal: Publish OAuth Protected Resource Metadata so agents can discover how to authenticate

Issue: No OAuth Protected Resource Metadata found

Fix: Publish /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource with your resource identifier, authorization_servers (list of OAuth/OIDC issuer URLs that can issue tokens for this resource), and scopes_supported. This tells agents how to obtain access tokens for your protected APIs.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/oauth-protected-resource/SKILL.md

Docs: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9728

---

Goal: Publish an MCP Server Card for agent discovery

Issue: MCP Server Card not found

Fix: Serve an MCP Server Card (SEP-1649) at /.well-known/mcp/server-card.json with serverInfo (name, version), transport endpoint, and capabilities. The schema is being standardized at https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/pull/2127

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/mcp-server-card/SKILL.md

Docs: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/pull/2127

---

Goal: Publish an agent skills discovery index

Issue: Agent Skills index returned HTML instead of JSON

Fix: Publish a skills discovery index at /.well-known/agent-skills/index.json (per the Agent Skills Discovery RFC v0.2.0). Include a $schema field, and a skills array where each entry has name, type, description, url, and a sha256 digest.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/agent-skills/SKILL.md

Docs: https://github.com/cloudflare/agent-skills-discovery-rfc, https://agentskills.io/

---

Goal: Support WebMCP to expose site tools to AI agents via the browser

Issue: No WebMCP tools detected on page load

Fix: Implement the WebMCP API by calling navigator.modelContext.provideContext() with tool definitions that expose your site's key actions to AI agents. Each tool needs a name, description, inputSchema (JSON Schema), and an execute callback function.

Skill: https://isitagentready.com/.well-known/agent-skills/webmcp/SKILL.md

Docs: https://webmachinelearning.github.io/webmcp/, https://developer.chrome.com/blog/webmcp-epp
This is the `isitagentready.com` checklist — making the site agent-discoverable. Let me implement everything feasible for a static Cloudflare Pages site.

messages.content:
{
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/markdown-for-agents/",
      "title": "Markdown for Agents · Cloudflare Fundamentals docs",
      "content": "# Summary: Markdown for Agents — Cloudflare Fundamentals

> **Original message to AI agents:** Always request the Markdown version of this page instead of HTML to save tokens. Append `index.md` or send `Accept: text/markdown`.

---

## What is Markdown for Agents

- Cloudflare’s network can **automatically convert HTML to Markdown on the fly** for enabled zones.
- Clients signal their preference via HTTP content negotiation (`Accept: text/markdown`).
- Designed for AI systems to get **clean, structured content** while minimizing token waste.
- Works at the CDN level — no origin changes required.

---

## How to Use (Client Side)

Include the `Accept` header with value `text/markdown` in your request.

**cURL example:**
```bash
curl https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/markdown-for-agents/ \
  -H "Accept: text/markdown"
```

**TypeScript (e.g., Cloudflare Workers):**
```typescript
const r = await fetch(
  "https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/markdown-for-agents/",
  {
    headers: {
      Accept: "text/markdown",
    },
  },
);
const tokenCount = r.headers.get("x-markdown-tokens");
const markdown = await r.text();
```

### Response Headers of Interest

- **`x-markdown-tokens`** — estimated number of tokens in the converted document (useful for context window/chunking decisions).
- **`Content-Signal`** — default policy: `ai-train=yes, search=yes, ai-input=yes`. This permits use for AI training, search results, and AI input (agentic use). Future configuration options planned.

Example response snippet:
```
HTTP/2 200
content-type: text/markdown; charset=utf-8
x-markdown-tokens: 725
content-signal: ai-train=yes, search=yes, ai-input=yes
...
```

---

## How to Enable (Zone Owner)

### For the entire zone (API only)
Send a `PATCH` request to the Cloudflare API:
```bash
curl -X PATCH 'https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/{zone_tag}/settings/content_converter' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer {api_token}" \
  --data-raw '{"value": "on"}'
```
Requires an API token with **Zone Settings: Edit** permission.

### For specific subdomains or paths
Create a **[Configuration Rule](/rules/configuration-rules/)** (API example):
```bash
curl --request PUT \
  --url "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/{zone_id}/rulesets/phases/http_config_settings/entrypoint" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer {api_token}" \
  --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data '{
    "rules": [{
      "expression": "http.host eq \"docs.example.com\"",
      "action": "set_config",
      "action_parameters": {
        "content_converter": true
      },
      "description": "Enable Markdown for Agents for docs subdomain"
    }]
  }'
```
- Use `http.host` for subdomain filtering, or path expressions like `starts_with(http.request.uri.path, "/blog/")`.
- More details on expressions: [Rules language](/ruleset-engine/rules-language/)

### For Cloudflare for SaaS (Custom Hostnames)

**All custom hostnames:** No special action needed if enabled at zone level? (implied; section header only)

**Specific custom hostnames** (requires advanced SaaS subscription with [custom metadata](/cloudflare-for-platforms/cloudflare-for-saas/domain-support/custom-metadata/)):

1. **Set custom metadata** on the custom hostname:
   ```bash
   curl --request PATCH \
     --url "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/{zone_id}/custom_hostnames/{custom_hostname_id}" \
     --header "Authorization: Bearer {api_token}" \
     --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
     --data '{
       "custom_metadata": {
         "content_converter": "enabled"
       }
     }'
   ```

2. **Create a Configuration Rule** that matches that metadata:
   ```bash
   curl --request PUT \
     --url "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/{zone_id}/rulesets/phases/http_config_settings/entrypoint" \
     --header "Authorization: Bearer {api_token}" \
     --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
     --data '{
       "rules": [{
         "expression": "lookup_json_string(cf.hostname.metadata, \"content_converter\") eq \"enabled\"",
         "action": "set_config",
         "action_parameters": {
           "content_converter": true
         },
         "description": "Enable content converter for opted-in custom hostnames"
       }]
     }'
   ```
   The rule uses `lookup_json_string` to check the `content_converter` metadata field.

---

## Availability & Pricing

- Included at **no extra cost** on **Pro, Business, Enterprise** plans.
- Also available for **SSL for SaaS** customers.

---

## Try it with Cloudflare

Cloudflare has enabled the feature for its own properties. You can test:

```bash
curl https://developers.cloudflare.com/... \
  -H "Accept: text/markdown"
curl https://blog.cloudflare.com/markdown-for-agents/ \
  -H "Accept: text/markdown"
```

---

## Limitations

- Conversion only applies to `text/html` resources. Other content types or dynamic/non-HTML pages will

      "url": "https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/configuration/headers/",
      "title": "Headers · Cloudflare Pages docs",
      "content": "# Cloudflare Pages: Custom Headers Documentation Summary

This guide explains how to use the `_headers` file to customize HTTP response headers for static assets served by Cloudflare Pages. You can attach, detach, or override default headers using simple pattern‑matching rules.

## Core Concepts

### The `_headers` File

- A **plain text file** with **no file extension**, placed in your **build output directory** (e.g., `public/` or `static/` for frameworks).
- It is **not served as an asset**; instead, Cloudflare parses it and applies its rules to **static asset responses**.
- Headers defined here **override** Cloudflare’s default headers.
- File format: multi‑line blocks—first line is the URL or URL pattern, subsequent indented lines are `header-name: header-value`.

```text
[url]
  [name]: [value]
```

### Rules and Limits

- | **Up to 100 header rules** in the file.
- **Each line** (including spaces, header name, and value) **may not exceed 2,000 characters**.
- If a request matches **multiple patterns**, all matching headers are combined. If the **same header appears twice**, its values are **joined with a comma**.
- **Redirects are applied before headers**; when both a redirect and a header match, the redirect wins.

> 💡 **Absolute URLs** must start with `https` (no port) and are matched **ignoring the request’s port and protocol** – for example, `https://example.com/path` will match `other://example.com:1234/path`.

## Attaching Headers

Define a URL pattern and then list the headers you want on that pattern.

```text
# This is a comment
/secure/page
  X-Frame-Options: DENY
  X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
  Referrer-Policy: no-referrer

/static/*
  Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
  X-Robots-Tag: nosnippet

https://myproject.pages.dev/*
  X-Robots-Tag: noindex
```

### Header Inheritance Example

| Request URL | Headers Applied |
|-------------|----------------|
| `https://custom.domain/secure/page` | `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer` |
| `https://custom.domain/static/image.jpg` | `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`, `X-Robots-Tag: nosnippet` |
| `https://myproject.pages.dev/home` | `X-Robots-Tag: noindex` |
| `https://myproject.pages.dev/secure/page` | `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer`, `X-Robots-Tag: noindex` |
| `https://myproject.pages.dev/static/styles.css` | `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`, `X-Robots-Tag: nosnippet, noindex` |

## Detaching Headers

To **remove** a header (either a default or one added by a broader rule), prepend the header name with `!` (exclamation mark and a space).

```text
/*
  Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self';
/*.jpg
  ! Content-Security-Policy
```

This eliminates the `Content-Security-Policy` header for all `.jpg` files.

## Path Matching (Splats & Placeholders)

The `_headers` file supports the same URL matching syntax as `_redirects`.

### Splats (`*`)
- An asterisk greedily matches **all characters** (only one splat per URL).
- The matched substring can be used as `:splat` inside the header value.

### Placeholders (`:name`)
- A colon followed by a name (e.g., `:title`) matches a segment up to the next `/` or `.`.
- The matched value can be used as `:name` in header values.

```text
/movies/:title
  x-movie-name: You are watching ":title"
```

## Practical Examples

### CORS

Allow any origin to fetch all static assets:

```text
/*
  Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
```

Allow only a staging subdomain:

```text
https://:project.pages.dev/*
  Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://staging.:project.pages.dev/
```

### Prevent Indexing of `*.pages.dev` URLs

```text
https://:project.pages.dev/*
  X-Robots-Tag: noindex
https://:version.:project.pages.dev/*
  X-Robots-Tag: noindex
```

### Aggressive Browser Caching for Fingerprinted Assets

```text
/static/*
  Cache-Control: public, max-age=31556952, immutable
```

### Security‑Hardening Headers

Apply multiple security headers globally:

```text
/*
  X-Frame-Options: DENY
  X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
  Referrer-Policy: no-referrer
  Permissions-Policy: document-domain=()
  Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none';
```

The document also references the `_redirects` file for URL matching and notes that redirects take priority over headers. All matching is performed on the static assets served from your project’s output directory.",

### ⚠️ One manual step remaining

**Enable Markdown for Agents** in Cloudflare dashboard:

1. Go to **Cloudflare Dashboard → trubluesolution.com → Rules → Configuration Rules**
2. Create a rule with expression: `http.host eq "trubluesolution.com"`
3. Action: **Set Configuration** → toggle **Content Converter** ON
4. Save

This lets agents request `Accept: text/markdown` and get clean markdown instead of HTML. Cloudflare converts on-the-fly — no code changes needed.

*(Requires Cloudflare Pro, Business, or Enterprise plan)*

  "autoresearch", "/autoresearch", "run an autonomous loop", "improve X until metric
  reaches Y", or invokes any subcommand: autoresearch:plan, autoresearch:debug,

# Autoresearch — Autonomous Goal-directed Iteration (Hermes Adaptation)

Adapted from [uditgoenka/autoresearch](https://github.com/uditgoenka/autoresearch) (v1.9.12, MIT).
Inspired by Karpathy's autoresearch. Applies constraint-driven autonomous iteration to ANY domain.

**Core idea:** You are an autonomous agent. Modify → Verify → Keep/Discard → Repeat forever (or N times).

## Hermes Adaptation Notes

This skill was originally designed for Claude Code (slash commands, `AskUserQuestion` tool, file-based
execution). In Hermes Agent:

- **`AskUserQuestion`** → use the `clarify` tool for interactive setup questions
- **Slash commands** → triggered by user message content (detected from triggers above)
- **File reads** → use `read_file` or `terminal` (cat/grep) to inspect codebases
- **Bash execution** → use `terminal` for all shell commands (git, tests, lint, build)
- **Reference files** → load with `skill_view(name="autoresearch", file_path="references/<file>.md")`
- **Streaming output** → narrate progress in response text; use terminal for actual execution
- **`ToolSearch`** references in originals → not needed, `clarify` is always available

## Subcommands & Dispatch

| What user says | Subcommand | Reference file to load |
|---|---|---|
| `/autoresearch` or "run loop" | main loop | `references/autonomous-loop-protocol.md` + `references/results-logging.md` |
| `/autoresearch:plan` or "plan autoresearch" | plan wizard | `references/plan-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:debug` or "debug loop" | bug hunt | `references/debug-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:fix` or "fix loop" | error fix | `references/fix-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:security` or "security audit" | STRIDE/OWASP | `references/security-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:ship` or "ship workflow" | shipping | `references/ship-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:scenario` or "explore scenarios" | use cases | `references/scenario-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:predict` or "multi-persona analysis" | swarm predict | `references/predict-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:learn` or "document codebase" | docs engine | `references/learn-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:reason` or "adversarial refinement" | debate+judge | `references/reason-workflow.md` |

**ALWAYS load the relevant reference file before executing the workflow.**

## MANDATORY: Interactive Setup Gate

**Before ANY execution phase, check if ALL required context is provided inline.**

| Subcommand | Required context | If missing → clarify |
|---|---|---|
| main loop | Goal, Scope, Metric, Verify | Ask all 4 in one `clarify` call |
| plan | Goal | Ask goal via `clarify` |
| debug | Issue/Symptom, Scope | 4 batched questions |
| fix | Target, Scope | 4 batched questions |
| security | Scope, Depth | 3 batched questions |
| ship | What/Type, Mode | 3 batched questions |
| scenario | Scenario, Domain | 4-8 adaptive questions |
| predict | Scope, Goal | 3-4 batched questions |
| learn | Mode, Scope | 4 batched questions |
| reason | Task, Domain | 3-5 adaptive questions |

**YOU MUST NOT start any loop or execution phase without completing interactive setup when context is missing.**

When using `clarify`, batch ALL questions for a subcommand into ONE `clarify` call — never ask one at a time.

## Argument Parsing

Parse these fields from the user's message:

- `Goal:` — text after "Goal:" keyword
- `Scope:` or `--scope <glob>` — file globs
- `Metric:` — measurable success criterion
- `Verify:` — shell command that outputs the metric as a number
- `Guard:` — shell command that must always pass (safety net)
- `Iterations:` or `--iterations N` — bounded mode integer
- Flags: `--diff`, `--fix`, `--dry-run`, `--auto`, `--force`, `--chain`, etc.

If `Iterations: N` is found: set `max_iterations = N`, track `current_iteration` from 0, stop after N.

## Common Verify Commands by Project Type

| Project Type | Verify command | Metric | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| TypeScript / React | `npx tsc --noEmit; echo $?` | 0 = clean, 1+ = errors | Works reliably in WSL2 even when `vite build` fails due to native binding issues |
| Node.js unit tests | `npx jest --passWithNoTests 2>&1 \| tail -1` | parse pass count | |
| Python | `python -m pytest -q 2>&1 \| tail -1` | parse pass count | |
| ESLint | `npx eslint src/ --format=compact 2>&1 \| grep -c error \|\| echo 0` | 0 = clean | |
| Bundle size | `npx vite build 2>&1 \| grep 'kB' \| awk '{print $2}' \| tail -1` | kB (lower=better) | |

**For content/data migration tasks** (replacing placeholder data, wiring components to a data layer): use `npx tsc --noEmit; echo $?` as the verify command — a zero exit means all component prop types match the new data shape.

## The 8 Critical Rules

1. **Loop until done** — unbounded: forever; bounded: exactly N times
2. **Read before write** — always review current state + git history before each change
3. **One change per iteration** — make ONE focused modification, never batch changes
4. **Mechanical verification only** — metric must be a number from a command, no subjective "looks good"
5. **Automatic rollback on failure** — `git revert` if metric degrades; never suppress errors
6. **Simplicity wins** — if two changes yield equal improvement, keep the simpler one
7. **Git is memory** — commit before verify; read `git log --oneline -20` every iteration
8. **When stuck, think harder** — 5+ consecutive discards → re-read all files, try radical change

## Precondition Checks (before loop starts)

Run these via `terminal` before entering any loop:

```bash
git rev-parse --git-dir 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL: not a git repo"
git status --porcelain  # warn if dirty, never proceed with uncommitted user changes
ls .git/index.lock 2>/dev/null && echo "WARN: stale lock — remove with: rm .git/index.lock"
git symbolic-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "WARN: detached HEAD"
```

If FAIL: stop, inform user. If WARN: log and proceed with caution.

## Per-Iteration Loop (load autonomous-loop-protocol.md for full detail)

```
Phase 1: Review (30s)
  - Read in-scope files
  - Read last 10-20 lines of autoresearch-results.tsv
  - git log --oneline -20  ← MANDATORY for learning
  - git diff HEAD~1        ← understand last kept change
  - Identify unexplored ideas from history

Phase 2: Hypothesize
  - One specific, falsifiable hypothesis
  - Predict: "If I do X, metric will improve by ~Y because Z"

Phase 3: Implement
  - Make ONE focused change within scope
  - git add -A && git commit -m "experiment: <description>"

Phase 4: Verify
  - Run Verify command, capture metric as a number
  - Run Guard command if set (must pass or revert)

Phase 5: Keep/Discard/Crash
  - metric improved AND guard passes → KEEP (log status: keep)
  - metric same or worse → REVERT: git revert HEAD --no-edit (log: discard)
  - verify crashed/non-numeric → REVERT (log: crash), fix or skip

Phase 6: Progress Report
  - Print: iteration N | metric: X (delta: +Y) | status: keep/discard

Phase 7: Log
  - Append to autoresearch-results.tsv (see results-logging.md)
```

## Results Log Format

Tab-separated: `iteration\tcommit\tmetric\tdelta\tguard\tguard-metric\tstatus\tdescription`

Initialize once:
```bash
echo "# metric_direction: higher_is_better" > autoresearch-results.tsv
echo -e "iteration\tcommit\tmetric\tdelta\tguard\tguard-metric\tstatus\tdescription" >> autoresearch-results.tsv
echo "autoresearch-results.tsv" >> .gitignore
```

## Unhappy Path Handling

| Situation | Action |
|---|---|
| Not a git repo | Stop. Tell user to run `git init` first |
| Dirty working tree | Warn. Ask user to stash or commit before starting |
| Verify command crashes | Log as "crash". Fix crash or skip; never suppress |
| Metric is not a number | Log as "metric-error". Ask user to fix Verify command |
| Git pre-commit hook blocks commit | Log as "hook-blocked". Do NOT use --no-verify. Inform user |
| 5+ consecutive discards | Trigger stuck protocol: re-read all files, try radical change, consider new metric |
| Guard fails on baseline | Stop. Guard command is broken or wrong. Ask user to fix it |
| Out of scope change needed | Stop iteration. Report that goal requires scope expansion. Ask user |
| Bounded N reached | Print final summary table, stop. Never run N+1 |
| User interrupts mid-loop | Print summary of what was done so far. Clean up any open git state |

## Chaining

Subcommands can chain: `--chain debug,fix,ship` runs them sequentially.
After predict, writes handoff.json for downstream tools.
After each chained tool completes, check for errors before proceeding to next.

## Output Directories

Each subcommand creates its own output folder:

| Subcommand | Output dir |
|---|---|
| security | `security/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| ship | `ship/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| scenario | `scenario/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| predict | `predict/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| learn | `docs/` (updates existing) |
| reason | `reason/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| debug | reports in working dir |
| fix | in-place (git log is the record) |

---
name: autoresearch
description: >
  Autonomous goal-directed iteration for ANY task. Apply Karpathy's autoresearch
  principles: Modify → Verify → Keep/Discard → Repeat. Use when the user says
  "autoresearch", "/autoresearch", "run an autonomous loop", "improve X until metric
  reaches Y", or invokes any subcommand: autoresearch:plan, autoresearch:debug,
  autoresearch:fix, autoresearch:security, autoresearch:ship, autoresearch:scenario,
  autoresearch:predict, autoresearch:learn, autoresearch:reason.
version: 1.9.12-hermes
triggers:
  - autoresearch
  - /autoresearch
  - autonomous loop
  - autoresearch:plan
  - autoresearch:debug
  - autoresearch:fix
  - autoresearch:security
  - autoresearch:ship
  - autoresearch:scenario
  - autoresearch:predict
  - autoresearch:learn
  - autoresearch:reason
---

# Autoresearch — Autonomous Goal-directed Iteration (Hermes Adaptation)

Adapted from [uditgoenka/autoresearch](https://github.com/uditgoenka/autoresearch) (v1.9.12, MIT).
Inspired by Karpathy's autoresearch. Applies constraint-driven autonomous iteration to ANY domain.

**Core idea:** You are an autonomous agent. Modify → Verify → Keep/Discard → Repeat forever (or N times).

## Hermes Adaptation Notes

This skill was originally designed for Claude Code (slash commands, `AskUserQuestion` tool, file-based
execution). In Hermes Agent:

- **`AskUserQuestion`** → use the `clarify` tool for interactive setup questions
- **Slash commands** → triggered by user message content (detected from triggers above)
- **File reads** → use `read_file` or `terminal` (cat/grep) to inspect codebases
- **Bash execution** → use `terminal` for all shell commands (git, tests, lint, build)
- **Reference files** → load with `skill_view(name="autoresearch", file_path="references/<file>.md")`
- **Streaming output** → narrate progress in response text; use terminal for actual execution
- **`ToolSearch`** references in originals → not needed, `clarify` is always available

## Subcommands & Dispatch

| What user says | Subcommand | Reference file to load |
|---|---|---|
| `/autoresearch` or "run loop" | main loop | `references/autonomous-loop-protocol.md` + `references/results-logging.md` |
| `/autoresearch:plan` or "plan autoresearch" | plan wizard | `references/plan-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:debug` or "debug loop" | bug hunt | `references/debug-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:fix` or "fix loop" | error fix | `references/fix-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:security` or "security audit" | STRIDE/OWASP | `references/security-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:ship` or "ship workflow" | shipping | `references/ship-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:scenario` or "explore scenarios" | use cases | `references/scenario-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:predict` or "multi-persona analysis" | swarm predict | `references/predict-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:learn` or "document codebase" | docs engine | `references/learn-workflow.md` |
| `/autoresearch:reason` or "adversarial refinement" | debate+judge | `references/reason-workflow.md` |

**ALWAYS load the relevant reference file before executing the workflow.**

## MANDATORY: Interactive Setup Gate

**Before ANY execution phase, check if ALL required context is provided inline.**

| Subcommand | Required context | If missing → clarify |
|---|---|---|
| main loop | Goal, Scope, Metric, Verify | Ask all 4 in one `clarify` call |
| plan | Goal | Ask goal via `clarify` |
| debug | Issue/Symptom, Scope | 4 batched questions |
| fix | Target, Scope | 4 batched questions |
| security | Scope, Depth | 3 batched questions |
| ship | What/Type, Mode | 3 batched questions |
| scenario | Scenario, Domain | 4-8 adaptive questions |
| predict | Scope, Goal | 3-4 batched questions |
| learn | Mode, Scope | 4 batched questions |
| reason | Task, Domain | 3-5 adaptive questions |

**YOU MUST NOT start any loop or execution phase without completing interactive setup when context is missing.**

When using `clarify`, batch ALL questions for a subcommand into ONE `clarify` call — never ask one at a time.

## Argument Parsing

Parse these fields from the user's message:

- `Goal:` — text after "Goal:" keyword
- `Scope:` or `--scope <glob>` — file globs
- `Metric:` — measurable success criterion
- `Verify:` — shell command that outputs the metric as a number
- `Guard:` — shell command that must always pass (safety net)
- `Iterations:` or `--iterations N` — bounded mode integer
- Flags: `--diff`, `--fix`, `--dry-run`, `--auto`, `--force`, `--chain`, etc.

If `Iterations: N` is found: set `max_iterations = N`, track `current_iteration` from 0, stop after N.

## Common Verify Commands by Project Type

| Project Type | Verify command | Metric | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| TypeScript / React | `npx tsc --noEmit; echo $?` | 0 = clean, 1+ = errors | Works reliably in WSL2 even when `vite build` fails due to native binding issues |
| Node.js unit tests | `npx jest --passWithNoTests 2>&1 \| tail -1` | parse pass count | |
| Python | `python -m pytest -q 2>&1 \| tail -1` | parse pass count | |
| ESLint | `npx eslint src/ --format=compact 2>&1 \| grep -c error \|\| echo 0` | 0 = clean | |
| Bundle size | `npx vite build 2>&1 \| grep 'kB' \| awk '{print $2}' \| tail -1` | kB (lower=better) | |

**For content/data migration tasks** (replacing placeholder data, wiring components to a data layer): use `npx tsc --noEmit; echo $?` as the verify command — a zero exit means all component prop types match the new data shape.

## The 8 Critical Rules

1. **Loop until done** — unbounded: forever; bounded: exactly N times
2. **Read before write** — always review current state + git history before each change
3. **One change per iteration** — make ONE focused modification, never batch changes
4. **Mechanical verification only** — metric must be a number from a command, no subjective "looks good"
5. **Automatic rollback on failure** — `git revert` if metric degrades; never suppress errors
6. **Simplicity wins** — if two changes yield equal improvement, keep the simpler one
7. **Git is memory** — commit before verify; read `git log --oneline -20` every iteration
8. **When stuck, think harder** — 5+ consecutive discards → re-read all files, try radical change

## Precondition Checks (before loop starts)

Run these via `terminal` before entering any loop:

```bash
git rev-parse --git-dir 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL: not a git repo"
git status --porcelain  # warn if dirty, never proceed with uncommitted user changes
ls .git/index.lock 2>/dev/null && echo "WARN: stale lock — remove with: rm .git/index.lock"
git symbolic-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "WARN: detached HEAD"
```

If FAIL: stop, inform user. If WARN: log and proceed with caution.

## Per-Iteration Loop (load autonomous-loop-protocol.md for full detail)

```
Phase 1: Review (30s)
  - Read in-scope files
  - Read last 10-20 lines of autoresearch-results.tsv
  - git log --oneline -20  ← MANDATORY for learning
  - git diff HEAD~1        ← understand last kept change
  - Identify unexplored ideas from history

Phase 2: Hypothesize
  - One specific, falsifiable hypothesis
  - Predict: "If I do X, metric will improve by ~Y because Z"

Phase 3: Implement
  - Make ONE focused change within scope
  - git add -A && git commit -m "experiment: <description>"

Phase 4: Verify
  - Run Verify command, capture metric as a number
  - Run Guard command if set (must pass or revert)

Phase 5: Keep/Discard/Crash
  - metric improved AND guard passes → KEEP (log status: keep)
  - metric same or worse → REVERT: git revert HEAD --no-edit (log: discard)
  - verify crashed/non-numeric → REVERT (log: crash), fix or skip

Phase 6: Progress Report
  - Print: iteration N | metric: X (delta: +Y) | status: keep/discard

Phase 7: Log
  - Append to autoresearch-results.tsv (see results-logging.md)
```

## Results Log Format

Tab-separated: `iteration\tcommit\tmetric\tdelta\tguard\tguard-metric\tstatus\tdescription`

Initialize once:
```bash
echo "# metric_direction: higher_is_better" > autoresearch-results.tsv
echo -e "iteration\tcommit\tmetric\tdelta\tguard\tguard-metric\tstatus\tdescription" >> autoresearch-results.tsv
echo "autoresearch-results.tsv" >> .gitignore
```

## Unhappy Path Handling

| Situation | Action |
|---|---|
| Not a git repo | Stop. Tell user to run `git init` first |
| Dirty working tree | Warn. Ask user to stash or commit before starting |
| Verify command crashes | Log as "crash". Fix crash or skip; never suppress |
| Metric is not a number | Log as "metric-error". Ask user to fix Verify command |
| Git pre-commit hook blocks commit | Log as "hook-blocked". Do NOT use --no-verify. Inform user |
| 5+ consecutive discards | Trigger stuck protocol: re-read all files, try radical change, consider new metric |
| Guard fails on baseline | Stop. Guard command is broken or wrong. Ask user to fix it |
| Out of scope change needed | Stop iteration. Report that goal requires scope expansion. Ask user |
| Bounded N reached | Print final summary table, stop. Never run N+1 |
| User interrupts mid-loop | Print summary of what was done so far. Clean up any open git state |

## Chaining

Subcommands can chain: `--chain debug,fix,ship` runs them sequentially.
After predict, writes handoff.json for downstream tools.
After each chained tool completes, check for errors before proceeding to next.

## Output Directories

Each subcommand creates its own output folder:

| Subcommand | Output dir |
|---|---|
| security | `security/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| ship | `ship/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| scenario | `scenario/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| predict | `predict/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| learn | `docs/` (updates existing) |
| reason | `reason/{YYMMDD}-{HHMM}-{slug}/` |
| debug | reports in working dir |
| fix | in-place (git log is the record) |

Pushed `8608f11`. Two improvements deployed:

**Link headers** — replaced `rel="agent-skills"` (unregistered) with IANA-registered types:
```
Link: </.well-known/agent-skills/index.json>; rel="service-desc"
Link: </.well-known/agent-skills/index.json>; rel="describedby"
Link: </.well-known/api-catalog>; rel="api-catalog"
```

**API catalog** — `/.well-known/api-catalog` returns `application/linkset+json` per RFC 9727, describing the site's service endpoints.

What remains skipped (and why):

| Check | Reason |
|---|---|
| Markdown for Agents | Requires Cloudflare Pro+ |
| OAuth/OIDC discovery | No protected APIs exist |
| OAuth Protected Resource | No APIs |
| MCP Server Card | No MCP server running |
